title: Level 445
wikitext:
{{Notice2|bg=#16374d|tc=#acd7e3|body=This page is designed to be read in Dark Mode.}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 445: "Waiting For You..." }}
{{CSS|content= .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(172,215,227,0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #9fc6d1; --theme-page-text-color: #2b2b2b; --theme-page-accent-mix-color: #43c2e8; --theme-community-header-color: #0f0f0f; --theme-link-color: #004794; --theme-accent-color: #158acf; --theme-accent-label-color: #ffffff; --theme-border-color: #4f95a8; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #0070bf; } .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(8,18,21,0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: #16374d; --theme-page-text-color: #ddeeff; --theme-page-accent-mix-color: #184766; --theme-community-header-color: #ffffff; --theme-link-color: #00bbff; --theme-accent-color: #138eba; --theme-accent-label-color: #ffffff; --theme-border-color: #1367b0; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: #0e2236; } }} {{Background|file=441back.webp}} Level 445 is the 446th level of the Backrooms. {{Class 0 |hexagon=1e |color-h=200 |color-s=80% |color-l=4% |header=Threat Index: Class |class=1e - Environmental |safety=Minor Mental Hazards |security=Unstable Geometry |entity=Devoid of Entities}} {{Blurimage|file=441main.webp|hovertext=The cold doesn't bother me anyway.|caption=An image of '''Level 445'''.|width=290px|align=right}} == '''''Description''''' == Level 445 consists of an expansive system of asphalt pathways dotted with sparse boreal forests situated at night. The level is permeated by a layer of fog which restricts vision over long distances. Despite the level lacking a day-night cycle and being locked in a constant state of night, there is a natural blueish-teal glow emanating for no apparent natural source. The climate within Level 445 maintains a constant, unchanging 14.2o Celsius. The only natural life forms within the level are the numerous petrified [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quercus_alba| White Oak] trees scattered around the environment. Despite the large presence of snow within the level, Level 445 lacks any form of precipitation or weather, although more snow mysteriously continues to materialize on the ground. The snow itself is chemically identical to any non-anomalous snow found in other levels, although notably it does not leave behind water when it evaporates. Beneath the snow in the level usually lies a deep layer of permafrost or asphalt if one is directly above a pathway concealed by the snow. Level 445 is heavily affected by [[Phenomenon 14|Shifting Geometry]], making interactions with other wanderers, even those one may enter simultaneously with, incredibly difficult. Once one is far enough into the distant fog, they are instantly lost to those outside of view. Although the physical geometry of the level is non-Euclidean, sounds within Level 445 still travel is if the geometry were Euclidean. This often results in sounds being heard relatively clearly over incredibly long distances, or sounds being dampened while at close range with the source. {{Blurimage|file=441build.jpeg|hovertext=A land of frost and ice.|caption=A building within '''Level 445'''.|width=290px|align=left}} === '''''Buildings & Structures'''''=== Scattered distantly around the sprawling paths of Level 445, one may find small storage buildings. The interiors of the storage building are almost entirely unfurnished and empty, although they appear to be well maintained and clean. While it may be tempting to shelter within the rooms of these structures, they lack any heating or insulation, usually resulting in lower temperatures within them than the outdoors. The geometry of the structures are highly stable and do not shift in layout. Although it is possible to use the buildings in order to attempt to coordinate a gathering on the level, the exteriors of the building still shift with the same instability present in the exterior of the level. Entering a building at one location will cause you to exit at a completely different location, although you will still have exited from the same building, giving the impression that the building had moved, although there are no detectable signs of when the location of a building shifts whilst one is inside of one. Other objects within Level 445 include scattered arrays of streetlights which strangely appear to lack functioning lighting systems, as well as small walls of chain-link fencing. In addition, one may find the occasional steel bench, untouched by the snow and oftentimes slightly warmer than its surrounding environment. Level 445 contains extremely shallow rivers and lakes, although they are almost always frozen solid. === '''''The Patience Effect'''''=== The Patience Effect, occasionally referred to simply as "Patience", is a unique phenomenon affecting those within Level 445. Whilst a wanderer is within Level 445, they will slowly begin feelings of isolation and the state of being lost, oftentimes exacerbated by the numerous physical properties of the level. While this phenomenon is developing, the wanderer will begin to show feelings of hope that someone will arrive at them, usually resulting in the wanderer patiently waiting; maintaining the hope that their period of seclusion will end. It is important to note that both effects do not develop at the same rate and that both effects develop slowly, oftentimes without the affected individual noticing. Wanderers with previous or concurrent feelings of depression, isolation, or loneliness are much more likely to fall to the effects of the level, and the feelings of hope are oftentimes weaker. Although the Patience Effect may be oppressive upon first contraction, it can be controlled with pure concentration and realization that one is under its effects. This realization is often healing to the affected individual, especially if they have previously struggled with these issues.
'''Loneliness'''
''This transcript was recorded from M.E.G. Agent █████ Hudson, who was on a scouting mission through Level 445 to locate exits.'' {{Underline|FFFFFF}} "It's eerily empty here." "There's nothing coming down from the sky but—the piles of snow on the ground keep building up, and the streetlights keep on glowing but without shining a light. It feels so ''artificial'' here. Just—everything seems like it's missing." ''(Agent Hudson notices a building in the distance and begins walking towards it.)'' "With every step forward, I feel like I have to pull my gun just a bit closer; I keep getting that feeling that something, anything, could jump out from the fog and do god knows what to me." "Then my hands shake and then I feel shivers through my skin, and then it hits me that I am completely alone." "Sometimes I wish something really was there."
== '''''Entities''''' == Level 445 has a distinct lack of entities. The cause of this is unknown, as there are not any explicit reasons why entities would not appear here. == '''''Colonies & Outposts''''' == Although it is possible for multiple wanderers to enter Level 445 simultaneously, the numerous geometrical properties, along with the dense fog and auditory distortions, make the creation of colonies or outposts nearly impossible. Thus, none currently exist or will be likely to exist.
'''Snowfall'''
''"The patient winterlands are an interesting event. It isn't some paradisal mirage, nor is some nightmarish destiny. It isn't a glorious finale, neither is it an entryway to something greater. It's a mundane plane of nothingness, and yet it carries an essence, a soul of some sort.''
''The wasteland is in a constant limbo of perfection and defectiveness. Everything is so perfectly tuned for your comfort: the old trees, the cozy benches, the beautiful blue haze, and yet you still feel empty, consumed by the everlasting cold.''
''The obstacles on the way to your greatness, they aren't grand climaxes of power and reaction, they are the slow burns, the thoughts at the back of your mind nudging ever so closer forward each second you waste. If you can push yourself beyond the small things, even a babylion mass of hindrance won't be able to stop you.''
''I don't care if you pass away the snow and the frost, but do know that it is an inevitability, whether you wait for me or not."''
== '''''Entrances & Exits'''''== '''''Entrances'''''
'''⇒''' Locating noticably cooler waters within the [[Level 173|Never-Ending River]] and sinking into them will cause one to reemerge in the snow of this level.
'''⇒''' Wandering too far into the outdoors of the [[Level 26|Otherworldly]] will lead here, especially if the fog resembles that of this level.
'''''Exits'''''
'''⇐''' The simplest way to exit Level 445 is to fall asleep, in which one will wake up inside the [[Level 245|Soviet Languor]].
'''⇐''' Gazing at the night sky for long enough has a chance of bringing one to the [[Level 306|Blue Lucidity]]. The amount of time required to perform this is unknown, but is theorized to be around 4 to 7 minutes.
'''⇐''' One may locate a seemingly short dirt path which may transport one into [[Level 680|Bezdna]] when traversed through.
'''⇐''' Realizing one's fabricated silence and willingly falling under its trance in an attempt of self-harm may bring one to [[Level 888|Inocentia]] through unknown means.

Written by [[User:VAARYON|vaaryon]], greenlit by [[User:SherlsF|SherlsF]], and originally created as Level -14 by Meg for The Backrooms: REDACTED, an amazing game on Roblox which you should absolutely check out.
Third page done! I can finally get image perms in the discord server now! Although this page is still based off of the original, a LOT of things were changed to make it more compatible with Fandom. Since Bezdna (TB:R's Level -15) doesn't exist here, it was replaced with Level 245 which is similar enough and works better with the main exit (which fun fact, was originally meant for Level 100). I began working on this page at 9:27 AM, January 24th, 2026 and finished at 2:19 AM, January 25th, 2026. This is also my first major attempt at developing my own CSS and Wikitext, as a lot of this stuff was heavily modified from other templates (no stolen wikitext!), which was really fun. Anyways shoutout to whoever is reading this for being extra sigma, and read my other pages! {{Audio|mono=yes|align=center|file=subzero.mp3|volume=0.05|secrettext=the silence dwarfs your voice}}
(NOTE: This page doesn't take any of the narrative elements from the game, it is simply a reimagining if it were to be its own official page.) |initial=
''You sit down on the bitter, cold bench and preach your somber lyrics:'' |final=
''"Is there someone out there waiting for me?"''
